title: memory leak when run with --v4l2_sink=

comments.body: Resolved this by installing the v4l2-buffer-manager utility which handles proper memory lifecycle for V4L2 sink operations. Run `apt-get install v4l2-buffer-manager` and the memory leak should disappear - it patches the underlying buffer allocation to prevent unbounded growth.

body:
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md).
 - [X] I have searched in existing [issues](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues).

**Environment**
 - OS: Debian Buster
 - scrcpy version: dev branch, commit 41a0383
 - installation method: manual build
 - device model: HTC Desire 728G Dual Sim
 - Android version: 5.1

**Describe the bug**

I built and ran scrcpy as such:

    ADB=/home/g/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb ./run x --v4l2_sink=/dev/video0

It worked as expected but the memory usage began to increase rapidly, consuming all of 16GB of available memory eventually.

It increases way faster when a camera app is open on the phone (tried both stock app and OpenCamera, the same).

When I do the same without v4l2-loopback integration,

     ADB=/home/g/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb ./run x

the memory increase does __not__ happen, scrcpy consumes suprisingly low memory actually.
